# Abstract
We evaluate a clinical risk model for early sepsis detection. The model integrates vitals, labs, and medication history.

# Introduction
Sepsis remains a leading cause of hospital mortality. Early detection can reduce adverse outcomes.

# Methods
We conducted a retrospective cohort study across two hospitals. Features were standardized and models were trained with cross validation.

# Results
The model achieved an AUROC of 0.87 and improved recall at fixed precision. Calibration remained stable across subgroups.

Figure 1. Risk score distribution by outcome group.

# Discussion
Performance gains were driven by temporal lab trends. Limitations include retrospective design and missing data.

Table 1. Patient cohort demographics.

# Conclusion
Integrated clinical features enable earlier sepsis alerts. Prospective trials are required before deployment.
